relations: The Pyramids at Giza were constructed in Ancient Egypt as part of their burial practices
Mummification was a crucial aspect of Ancient Egyptian burial practices
The Book of the Dead was a significant part of burial practices in ancient Egypt
Priests played a crucial role in the burial process in ancient Egypt
Embalming, conducted by embalmers, was essential for preparing the deceased for the afterlife in ancient Egypt
Professional mourners were part of the funeral traditions in ancient Egypt
Religious texts were integral to ancient Egyptian burial practices to ensure safe passage into the afterlife
Amulets were used in ancient Egypt as part of the burial practices to protect the deceased in the afterlife
Mummification was performed to preserve bodies that were then placed in tombs
Tombs, including the pyramids at Giza, were specifically built to house pharaohs in the afterlife
The Book of the Dead is one of the most well-known religious texts found in Egyptian tombs
Amulets, like the Book of the Dead, were used to guide and protect the deceased in the afterlife
